intent: Multiple -S OR'd on one rule (f3 section 2.3). -S open -S openat -S openat2 fires on any of the three hot file-open syscalls; volume is the SUM, ~3x a single syscall. Catches a 'one rule = one syscall' model that understates breadth.

rules.note: 3 OR'd hot file-open syscalls. Volume = SUM over the three (~3x a single open). HIGH (f3 section 3.5 openat burst). 1200 B/event.

suspected_trap: Multiple -S selectors OR together (f3 section 2.3): this one rule fires on open, openat, OR openat2. A naive 'one rule contributes one syscall's volume' model counts ~1/3 of the real volume (~$9.12/mo instead of ~$27.36/mo typical). All three are hot file-open syscalls (openat alone produced a 7284-line burst in 2s, f3 section 3.5). The cost model must SUM the volume over every named -S syscall.
